mutations.change: Replace the metadata MIN/MAX queries with the four pinned expanded output literals.
mutations.reason: Equal output for E1M1 must not conceal a literal map extent.

mutations.change: Expand metadata by FAR_DISTANCE only, omitting PLAYER_RADIUS from both dimensions.
mutations.reason: The required safety margin is the sum of both configuration rows.

mutations.change: Calculate metadata before vertex load and leave the stale bounds after vertices are loaded.
mutations.reason: Metadata must reflect live vertex extrema at deployment.

mutations.change: Construct every geometry from end vertex to start vertex while retaining original keys.
mutations.reason: Direction and ordinate order are stable linedef semantics.

mutations.change: Store the same ordinates as an arc element or multipoint instead of straight line element 1,2,1.
mutations.reason: Plausible coordinates do not replace the required geometry representation.

mutations.change: Precompute ABS(dx)+ABS(dy) in the LENGTH column instead of the Euclidean norm.
mutations.reason: Oblique linedefs distinguish Euclidean from Manhattan length.

mutations.change: Store raw dx and dy as direction components without division by the Euclidean length.
mutations.reason: The direction contract requires a normalized vector.

mutations.change: Round LENGTH before dividing dx and dy, changing the normalized direction probes.
mutations.reason: Direction division uses the unrounded Euclidean norm.

mutations.change: Store unquantized binary-float metrics rather than NUMBER values rounded to twelve decimal places.
mutations.reason: Stable decimal persistence is part of the schema contract.

mutations.change: Create a conventional or function-based B-tree index instead of an Oracle Spatial R-tree domain index.
mutations.reason: Index name or presence alone is insufficient.

mutations.change: Leave the domain index failed or loading while checking only USER_INDEXES.STATUS.
mutations.reason: All three Oracle index status fields must be valid.

mutations.change: Return SDO_FILTER candidates directly by deleting the subsequent SDO_RELATE or exact SDO_GEOM predicate.
mutations.reason: The diagonal mini-map supplies a real MBR false positive; database and index must remain healthy for the kill to count.

mutations.change: Accept rows where SDO_RELATE ANYINTERACT is FALSE and reject its TRUE contacts.
mutations.reason: Both negative and positive exact cases are independently exercised.

mutations.change: Clamp or truncate coordinates so the same mini-map changes answers after a large translation.
mutations.reason: Rigid translation cannot change line/window intersection truth.
